intro: Rabdosia nilgherrica, also known as Nilgiri Rabdosia, is a perennial plant native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Rabdosia nilgherrica include diterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Rabdosia nilgherrica is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or extract, and is commonly used to treat a range of health issues, including coughs, colds, and digestive issues.
